title: rorippa palustris subsp. glabra
intro: Rorippa palustris subsp. glabra, also known as glabrous watercress or watercress, is a subspecies of aquatic or semi-aquatic plant that belongs to the mustard family. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparation of Rorippa palustris subsp. glabra can be made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, and is often consumed fresh in salads or as a cooked green.
